evidence: "Kakushigoto is a fun series... a simple, mostly funny story about family dedication and manga production."
"The quintessential high school comedy/drama/romance anime every weeb must see."
"Mardock Scramble fall short in delivery... character development is lacking."
"At times its brilliant, at times its annoying but it keeps you coming back to see what happens next."
"Filled with kiddy fluff, the story gets off track and never really resolves."
"The animation is very good as is the premise but it suffers from a few overdone scenes..."
"The hand work part of the animation and backgrounds are quite good and at time striking..."
"If you like My Hero Academia and Full Metal Alchemist Brotherhood, try this one."
"Weathering with You is from the writer/creator of Your Name..."
"The artwork is impressive, the characters develop into noble antagonists and you will shed a tear."
"The animation and artwork are striking along with its action sequences graphic violence."
"Keep a box of tissues nearby at the end."
"If the goal of art is to affect the viewer and leave them with an impression, this does that as much as any classic literature does."

summary: The user enjoys a variety of anime genres, particularly drama, romance, and action, with a preference for simpler narratives that evoke emotional responses. They appreciate good animation quality but are not overly concerned with production polish, indicating a balanced taste in storytelling and visual artistry.
